q: The pitch of a note is determined by its ___.
Greater frequency in a sound wave results in a ___.
Which property of a wave determines its loudness?
A greater amplitude produces a ___ note.
Larger amplitude is produced when ___ energy is used.
___ is the characteristic that distinguishes sounds of the same loudness and same pitch.
What is another term used for the quality of a sound?
The timbre of a sound depends on its ___.
Waveform varies according to the number of ___.
Violin notes are richer because they have more ___.
___ is the energy transmitted per second per unit area held normally to direction of sound.
In the intensity formula, what does $\\rho$ represent?
The intensity of a wave is directly proportional to the ___.
___ is a sensation and depends on the observer.
To human ears, the loudness of a sound is ___.
Normally, greater intensity results in greater ___.
Which of the following does NOT affect the quality of sound?
How high a note is describes its ___.
The intensity of sound is energy transmitted per ___ per unit area.
The area used in defining intensity is held ___ to the direction of sound.
Which factor is proportional to intensity according to the relation $I \\propto A^2$?
In the formula $I = \\frac{1}{2} \\rho v \\omega^2 A^2$, $v$ represents ___.
Which property allows one to distinguish a flute from a guitar playing the same note?
Waveform is affected by the number of harmonics or ___.
A sound perceived as 'loud' by one person and 'soft' by another indicates that loudness is ___.
Which of the following is an objective measure of energy flow?
Angular frequency is denoted by which symbol in the intensity formula?
The frequency of a note determines its ___.
The amplitude of a wave determines its ___.
The waveform of a sound determines its ___.
Intensity is defined as energy per second per ___.
If you use more energy to produce a sound, you increase its ___.
Timbre varies with the number of overtones or ___.
The sensation of sound that depends on the observer is ___.
Pitch corresponds to the ___ of the wave.
Higher frequency notes have a ___.
The unit area in intensity is held ___ to sound direction.
Intensity is proportional to the square of ___.
___ varies with the number of overtones or harmonics.
The richness of musical notes is associated with ___.
Loudness is subjective and depends on the ___.
The intensity formula includes the square of the ___.
A note's 'highness' depends on ___.
Intensity is energy per ___ per unit area.
The square of amplitude is proportional to ___.
The characteristic of 'timbre' depends on ___.
Greater frequency leads to higher ___.
Greater amplitude leads to louder ___.
The energy transmitted per second per unit area is ___.
Subjectivity is a property of ___.
